product_title: Eyebrow Pen, Microblading Eyebrows, 4 Points Eyebrow Pencil, Creates Lasting Make-Up Professional Natural Looking Eyebrows, Cover Sparse Areas, Daily Waterproof Eyebrow Pen with Gift
clean_review_title: Good product but quantity misleading: Only one pen included!
clean_review_text: Is it just me? I have purchased this exact product several times--happily--in dark brown, grey brown, and chestnut (red brown). My eyebrows are, like my natural hair, about a level 4 or 5 dark brown, and the grey brown is probably the closest, though the other colors are handy for changes of color to hair and makeup. My brows are thinning, but a little filling in does the trick, and these pens are my favorite tool for the job, as they both color in where you need solid filling and can be used to make feathery strokes for a more natural brow look. This pen came with a useless little razor and three eyebrow "templates," only one of which I might consider useful (if you are new to shaping your brows). The problem: In the past I have received (I see now from other sellers) at least two or three pens for $6.99 to $8.99. I can't believe I paid over $8 for just one pen from this seller "Generies." Going back over the seller page, I see that indeed, by the pictures it appears that one will receive two pens. A sneaky little trick, showing the same photos as the sellers with better deals, but neglecting to include the "2 CT." If you are busy or distracted in the least, you might fall for it, as did I--and doubtless, as have many customers. I really dislike writing negative comments, but am doing this to save others the trouble and disappointment of feeling ripped off. Needless to say, I will be buying this product from other sellers in future--the ones with the more reasonable--and honest--deals.
original_review_title: Good product but quantity misleading: Only one pen included!

product_title: Air King AK100L Exhaust Fan, Clear
clean_review_text: Pretty disappointed with this purchase. Spent a lot of time reading reviews. There are a lot of 3 star exhaust fans out there and this was one of the few that received 4+. Was disappointed the moment it arrived. Everything about it is chincy. All 4 metal brackets were bent. It was a breeze to bend them back by hand because the metal was so soft (mind you, these are supposed to be the "sturdy" brackets to mount the unit to your joist). The internal fan unit was somehow installed crooked in the housing when I pulled it from the box? It was like somebody sat on it, causing it to be pushed into the housing on a 45 degree angle...bending some of the internal parts. Had to pull it out and rebend to get it to sit flush. The instructions show 3 screws holding the motor to the fan...but there was only 1 screw. And it's not as though they just forgot some other screws...there weren't even other holes for the other screws! Despite all of these things, I still brought it up to the attic to install. Once I began the electrical work, I just couldn't take it. The box is SO tiny, it is impossible to fit all of your connections in there...even if you had a 12/2 and 12/3! Needless to say, I returned it, spent the extra $95 for a Panasonic and couldn't be happier. The second I pulled the Panasonic from the box, I knew it was money well-spent. Solid, straight, oversized electrical box. Unfortunately, this Air King model just didn't cut it. Actually, it didn't even come close.
llm_complaint_title: Bent brackets crooked fan missing screws

I reset the house circuit breaker, but the stove would not come on. None of the electrics now work, including, of course, the igniters. I will not repair this range. product_title: Keurig My K-Cup Universal Reusable K-Cup Pod Coffee Filter, Compatible with Keurig Classic/1.0 K-Cup Pod Coffee Makers, 1 Count, White
clean_review_text: I ordered two of these to replace a couple of older ones because the lids cracked in half (see photo). I've had four or five of these lids break along the left side, up to the red disc. Happens every time after a few months of use. Anyway, I haven't had the new ones long enough to see if they too will break, but I'm not a fan of the redesign. The new design has a wire basket with a lid, like the old one, but the basket then goes inside a plastic shell with a single hole in the bottom. So preparing one for use requires two steps instead of one, and cleaning is a pain. The old basket had a "handle" that I used to rap on the edge of a bowl to shake the grounds loose (I save my grounds for gardening). The new basket lacks the handle and is generally sort of flimsy, making it difficult to empty. Also, the new design allows you to make either a cup or a carafe. Okay, not a bad idea, except that now you have to go through another menu, in which you must select either a picture of a carafe or a number, 1-6; then you get to another screen where you can select the size. There's really no explanation of what the numbers mean (I know, it's probably in the Keurig manual somewhere) so we just guess and then see what we get. The whole thing is annoying. Amazon is famous for reducing transactional friction; it's disappointing when a company goes out of its way to increase friction. On the plus side, a cup of coffee seems to brew more quickly. Haven't actually timed the two models, but it feels faster. Regarding Keurig customer service: I recently posted a question about the cracking lids, and someone from Keurig suggested that I call customer support. I did, and it was a waste of time. The rep took all my personal info down and said he would pass it on to someone else who might be able to do something about replacing the broken lids. Or not, he wasn't sure. That was the last I've heard from them.
llm_complaint_title: Redesign hard to clean and empty

clean_review_title: Small bath bombs with terrible performance & it stains my skin
clean_review_text: [[VIDEOID:491e386908deb8251c574f6eb0c8758f]] There are 12 individually wrapped smal bath bombs in this set. The fragrances are, orange, lavender, frankincense, rosemary, mint, lemongrass, vanilla, seasalt, kiwi, rose, coconut, and papaya. I tried the papaya. When it was first plunged into the water (see video) there was fizz and no suds. Before I could even put the foot bath onto the floor to bathe my feet, the entire bomb fizzed out and dissolved into tang colored water less than 30 seconds later (see photo). Also, the fragrance was hardly detectable even though they smell good in the package. Since I personally do not like the color rings caused by bath bombs in my bath, I used this in a foot bath. The water turned an orange color and that color adhered to my skin and stained my towels but did not stain my foot bath. In summary, these bombs perform terribly, are staining, and are barely fragrant in the bath. The current price of $19.99 is expensive but still competitively priced for this as it comes to $1.67 per bath bomb, which is about what I would expect to pay for bombs of this size. However, at that price, I also expect these to perform and they do not. These are super lackluster and an utter disappointment. I am surprised others rated this 4 or 5 stars. I doubt the other reviewers even tried this in the bath. In the box they look impressive but If they had used at least one, they most likely would not be so impressed.

clean_review_text: I have been using the oil-free eye makeup remover for several years, so I thought I would try this. I don't recommend it. clean_review_title: Blocks air flow more than it should
clean_review_text: This is unlike the actual LG LT120F filter. The LG filter is a solid carbon block with holes allowing airflow. This product is a mesh with a fabric backing. Proper airflow is essential to the functioning of a refrigerator as air circulates from the freezer to the refrigerator and back to the freezer. The circulation fan is not powerful, and I am concerned that this 3rd-party filter will block the airflow. Air has to somehow make it through this fabric. The fabric is glued to the plastic and cannot easily be removed. LG refrigerators have enough problems already. The goal of this filter is to remove odors. Carbon does that. It is not designed to clean dust out of the air. So having a fabric filter is inappropriate for this product. Other third-party filters look like the LG one, where you can see through the holes to the other side.

clean_review_text: I have tasted quite a few different wheatgrass drinks over the years. I even still have a wheatgrass juicer somewhere in my garage that I spent a few hundred dollars on. While wheatgrass may be very healthy for you, it honestly tastes horrible. You know that smell when the neighbor finishes mowing their lawn? That smell is exactly what whatgrass tastes like, minus the scent of gasoline. I have tried using or juicing wheatgrass at various points in my life, but those phases never seem to last long. Besides the horrible taste it causes me an upset stomach and leaves a very horrible taste in my mouth. I can't say as this product tastes any different than other powders or fresh juiced wheatgrass that I have had in the past. product_title: Ultra Durable 154701001 Dishwasher Splash Shield Replacement Part by BlueStars - Exact Fit for Frigidaire & Kenmore Dishwashers - Replaces 1465007 154685101 AP4338941 PS2203346 - Pack of 2
clean_review_text: These splash shields look and fit exactly like the factory originals. The only problem I've had is that they seem to be stiffer than the original version. This is an issue because of the flawed design of the bottom rack in the Whirlpool/Maytag/etc. dishwasher where the rollers press down on the splash shields when the rack is rolled in and out to load/unload. This pressure seems to be why these splash shields fail so often. Since these don't flex as well as the originals, they are prone to be dislodged when the rack rolls over them. This can cause leakage. It can also cause the shields to fail even sooner since they don't flex as well. As a matter of interest, I recently checked the current models of the Whirlpool, etc. dishwashers and they don't use this splash shield at all but have redesigned the door and the door seal. I guess the engineers realized this was a constantly failing part.

You must keep the packaging to return the items for repair and they will not ship out certain items even if you pay for them (ie: compressor). This unit costs over $500.
